county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Brazos County formed 1841 from Robertson, Washington. County seat is Bryan. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Bryan. Central Texas, home to Texas A&M University. Bryan-College Station area. Check Robertson and Washington counties for pre-1841 records. Texas A&M University archives and Cushing Library hold regional manuscripts. Freedmen's Bureau records for the area. Railroad records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Brazos County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1841; County Clerk in Bryan holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Brazos County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1841 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Brazos County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1841. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Brazos County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1841. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Brazos County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Brazos County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Brazos County Enslaved Persons Records -- TSLAC / County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Brazos County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Brazos County Land Grants
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Republic of Texas and early statehood land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Brazos County. GLO archives are online with searchable databases. Texas managed its own public lands (unique among states). Empresario colony records if applicable.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Brewster County formed 1887 from Presidio. County seat is Alpine. West Texas / Permian Basin region with ranching, oil, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Alpine. Trans-Pecos county — largest county in Texas by area. Big Bend region. Archives of the Big Bend at Sul Ross State University. Brewster County Historical Commission. Mexican border crossing records. Check Presidio County for pre-1887 records. Ranching and mining records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Brewster County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1887; County Clerk in Alpine holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Briscoe County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Silverton. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Silverton. Panhandle county organized 1892. Ranching heritage — JA Ranch territory. Briscoe County Museum in Silverton. Check Bexar County for pre-organization records. Sparse early population.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Brooks County formed 1911 from Hidalgo, Starr. County seat is Falfurrias. South Texas / Rio Grande Valley region with Spanish colonial and Mexican land grant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Falfurrias. South Texas ranching county. Created from Hidalgo and Starr — check those for pre-1911 records. Strong Mexican-American heritage. Heritage Museum in Falfurrias. Death Valley of Texas (migrant history). Oil exploration from 1930s.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Brooks County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1911; County Clerk in Falfurrias holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Brown County formed 1856 from Comanche, Travis. County seat is Brownwood. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Brownwood. Central Texas county at the edge of the frontier. Brown County Museum of History. Camp Bowie (WWI and WWII) military records at NARA. Pecan Bayou area settlement. Check Comanche and Travis for pre-1856 records. Howard Payne University archives.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Burleson County formed 1846 from Milam. County seat is Caldwell. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Caldwell. Central Texas farming county. Czech and German immigrant communities — check Catholic and Lutheran church records. Burleson County Historical Society. Check Milam County for pre-1846 records. Freedmen's Bureau records for African American families.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Burnet County formed 1852 from Bell, Travis, Williamson. County seat is Burnet. Texas Hill Country region with German, Czech, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Burnet. Hill Country county. Fort Croghan Museum (frontier military post). Burnet County Heritage Society. German and Scotch-Irish settlers. Granite quarrying records (Granite Mountain). Check Bell, Travis, and Williamson for pre-1852 records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Caldwell County formed 1848 from Gonzales. County seat is Lockhart. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Lockhart. Central Texas county. Caldwell County Museum in Lockhart. Strong African American and Mexican-American communities. Cotton farming records. Check Gonzales County for pre-1848 records. Chisholm Trail cattle drive era. Lockhart BBQ heritage reflects diverse community.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Calhoun County formed 1846 from Jackson, Matagorda, Victoria. County seat is Port Lavaca. Gulf Coast region with port, plantation, and petrochemical heritage. BURNED COUNTY: 1886 hurricane and fire destroyed records.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Port Lavaca. 1886 hurricane and fire destroyed records — significant gaps in early documentation. Gulf Coast county. Prince Carl of Solms-Braunfels landed German immigrants at Indianola (1844-1846, now ghost town). Indianola was devastated by 1875 and 1886 hurricanes. Check Jackson, Matagorda, Victoria for pre-1846 records. Maritime records at NARA.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Calhoun County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1846; County Clerk in Port Lavaca holds deed books
